name: Metformin disuse and re-ambulation older-adult study
summary: A two-arm controlled study testing metformin during a two-week run-in and five days of bed rest, followed by metformin withdrawal during seven days of recovery.
population: Healthy male and female adults aged 60 years and older with BMI under 30

trial_details.horizon_note: ClinicalTrials.gov lists the parent registry as active, not recruiting, with no posted registry results and estimated 2026-11-01 completion. The published older-adult bed-rest result is already in the evidence layer, so this record remains a completed evidence study while the parent registry should be rechecked after completion.
trial_details.why_it_matters: Results matter for Senomorphics because they can show whether metformin changes skeletal-muscle senescence/SASP markers and recovery signals in older adults after bed rest. That is still narrower than proof of broad aging reversal.
